r: ʻain, ʻain-ě-mǒhmala, ʻain-ě-gair-manqūt̤a, alif, laʻl, lāl, hamza
def: (called ʻain-ě-mǒhmala, or ʻain-ě-gair-manqūt̤a), is the twenty-fourth letter of the Urdū alphabet (the eighteenth of the Arabic). This letter is peculiarly Arabic or Semitic, and occurs only in such Hindūstānī words as are borrowed from the Arabic, and in the secondary formations from these; or in the arabicized form of Persian words in which it has replaced an original alif (e.g. لعل laʻl, for لال lāl). It is a strong (but, to Europeans and most other people, unpronounceable) guttural, related in its nature to the hamza, and ḥ; and is described as produced by a smart compression of the fauces and forcible emission of breath. By the common people of India it is sounded like alif. As a numeral it stands for 70.
more: called عين مـﮩمله or عين غيرمنقوطه the 18th letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not occur in pure Persian and has no analogous character in Sanskrit. It is one of the radical letters. In abjad it stands for 70.

the eighteenth letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not occur in pure Persian, and has no analagous character in Sanskrit. It is one of the guttural letters, being formed in the lower part of the throat, by a compression of the fauces stronger and deeper than that used in the formation of hamza. Its sound has been compared to the voice of a calf calling its mother, or to that of a person who is making some painful exertion. It is one of the radical letters. In the denotation of numbers by the Arabic letters, it stands for 70. In Persian words introduced into Arabic it is substituted for ا as لعل for لال.

(f. -ī), This-like, such, of this sort; like ( = sā, in which case it is subjoined to the accusatival base of a pronoun, and to the acc. or gen. case of a substantive, e.g. āp-aisā 'aqlmand, 'a wise man like you'; mard-aisā, 'like a man'; par-kī aisī lakīroṅ ke rūp, 'in the form of feathery streaks'); in such a manner, in this wise, thus, so; after this sort, of a piece; — as though, as if; — so much, this much ( = itnā)
more: adj. S. ईदृश, Pr. eriso , Pālī. ediso such; H. is sā like this. 1. Like; similar; such; resembling; twin ( joṛā ). 1. Aise ādmī kam hote haiṅ . 2. Khanjadyā kī māṅ masurī kī dāl as pakāwaīt rahe, ki murgī ke suruā ke māt karaīt rahei . Bhoj. Prov. Khanjadyā's mother used to prepare such a delicious mess of masūr dāl (pulse) that chicken soup was nothing to it. (Said of his late wife to his new spouse who is a bad hand at cooking). 2. Matching; corresponding; analogous; parallel. 3. Of this kind or sort; of a piece. Khānā jahāṅ thā baṭṭā, wāṅ jā-ke dhakke khāë. Kambakht ko jo khānā aksar milā to aisā . On Poverty. Nazīr. And should he go where bread is served out free, he is pushed out, A dinner served out to the poor is oft a serving out.

adv. 1. After this manner; in this way; so; as. 1. Bandī jab shādī kartī hai, aisī hī kartī hai . Wom. Prov. Saying. When I marry my children it is always so (a grand affair)! 2. Aise gaë, jaise gadhe ke sir se sīṅg . Prov. Clean gone as horns from the head of a donkey. 3. Aisā gayā, jaise mahfil meṅ se jūtā . Prov. Vanished as shoes from an assembly. 2. As though; as if; belike; likely. 3. So much; this much; to such an extent (itnā).

def: n. m. aisī-taisī , n. f. S. तादृस्, Pr. tāriso . Such a one; a bad lot; a shady card; one who is no better than she should be; a scoundrel; one accursed. 1. Ye to jaldī se kahīṅ, yā Rab, mare, Aisā-taisā ho, jo byāh ab phir kare! Saudā. O God! may this one only quickly die somehow! May I be such a one if 'gain I wed, I vow! 2. Koī aisā-taisā yāṅ āwe to sahī! Dare such a scoundrel come here! aisā na ho, Khudā na kare ; P. mabādā, Khudā na khāstah , intj. Lest; may it not be; Heaven preserve us; God forbid.

adj. 1. Passable; indifferent; so so; middling; mediocre . 2. Trifling; trivial; insignificant; of little moment or worth. Maiṅ kyā aisā-waisā hūṅ jo dabūṅ . 3. Inferior; below par; paltry; petty. 4. Miserable; wretched; faint; feeble. 5. Foul; abusive; bad; obscene; indecent. Aisī waisī bāt mat kaho . 6. Evil; wrong; ill. Aisī-waisī bāt ho jāëgī, to tumhāre sir paṛegī. aisā-waisā honā, yūṅ tūṅ honā , v. n. (Wom). To suffer injury; to die. (A curse). Tumhāre ghar-ke sab aise waise ho jāëṅ! Wom.
adv. And so forth; etcetera. aise aise , adj. Such like; such as; similar. Aise aisoṅ ko maiṅ samajhtā kyā hūṅ!
(dakh.) In the mean time.

ety: inf. n. of عيش 'to live'; 'to live a pleasant life'
def: s.m. (orig.) 'Life; animal life'; a life of pleasure and enjoyment, pleasure, delight, luxury; gratification of the appetites, sensuality; carnal intercourse
more: n. m. 1. Luxury; a bed of roses; a bed of down ( ashrat ). 2. Animal gratification; sexual intercourse. aish uṛānā, yā k. , v. n. 1. To enjoy oneself. 2. To riot and revel in sensual enjoyments. aish ke bande, aishī-bande . Sensualists; Sybarites. aish mahal , n. m. The inner or female apartments of the house of a person of wealth or rank. aish o ashrat, aish o nishāt , n. Sensual pleasures; voluptuousness; debauch; orgies ( raṅg ralyāṅ ). aish, yā aish o ashrat se guzarnā , v. n. To live well; pass one's days in luxurious enjoyment; enjoy oneself.
s. m. Pleasure, delight. عيش گاه aﻌish-gāh, s. f. Place of merriment. عيش محل aﻌish-maḥall, Banqueting-house.
subs: or ʻaish karnā, v.n. To enjoy oneself; to revel in pleasure, or in sensual enjoyments
s.m. The slave of pleasure; a sensualist, a sybarite
s.f. or ʻaish-maḥal, s.m. Place or house of enjoyment or pleasure; — ʻaish-maḥal, Banqueting-house; — the inner or women's apartments of the house of a person of wealth or rank
s.m. Joy and delight, pleasure, merriment, excessive pleasure
s.f. or ʻaish-o-nashāt̤, s.m. Pleasantness of life; luxurious enjoyment; sensual pleasures, voluptuousness, &c.

wety: From آج (āj, “today”) + کَل (kal, “tomorrow; yesterday”). Compare Punjabi اَجّ کَلّ (ajj kall) / ਅੱਜਕੱਲ (ajjakalla).

def: adv. 1. To-day or to-morrow; in a few days; soon; "to-morrow and to-morrow". 1. Jān nā khāo, āj kal meṅ de dūṅgā . 2. Achchhā nahīṅ hai Mīr kā ahwāl in dinoṅ, Gālib ki ho chukegā yeh līmār āj kal . Mir. Mir's state of health in these days is not good at all—snuffed out. This sick man's like to be, to-morrow or to-day about. 3. Horī kā mahīno. saiyāṅ, der mat karyo, Mo ko āj kal sab maṅgb/?/ë de re; Mo ko chuṛyo kā j/?/ṛa, saīyāṅ lāë dere . Rus. Song. It is the month of Holī , husband, be not late, Get all things for me for these festive days, A set of bangles bring me, husband mine. 2. In these days; now-a-days; modern times. Āj kal bhāo charhā huā hai . 3. Of late; recently. Āj kal hī kā zikr hai. āj kal kar'nā, āj kal batā'nā , v. n. 1. To procrastinate; put off from day to day; delay; dilly dally; dally with. 2. To shilly shally; evade; shuffle. Woh taqāzā karte haiṅ, aur maiṅ āj kal kar rahā hāṅ . Gālib. āj kal ke,—kā,—kī , adj. Of the present day, age, or generation. Āj kal ke laṛke to apne muṅh se kahte haiṅ "hamārā byāh kar do." Kahānī .

adv. 1. Hitherto; to this day; as yet; till now; up to this time. 1. Sab ko neotal, keu na byāhal, Aja-hūṅ ādh kuṅār . Kabīr. She calleth all, but weddeth none. A virgin from the first she keeps. (Said of māyā , or the illusions of this world). 2. Āj-hūṅ lag mukh dikhātā naīn apas kā, Sajan mujh hāl suṅ kyā be-khabar hai! Walī. Till now he has not shewn his face to me, How heedless of my state my loved one seems! 2. Even now; just now; immediately; this very day; even to-day. āj fajar , adv. This morning. āj kidhar kā chāṅd hai? lit . On which side is the moon? Wonder of wonders! what a wonder! Āj kidhar kā chāṅd hai, jo tum idhar ā nīkle?
adv. To-day, this day; this time, now
more: adv. S. अद्य Pr. and Pālī. ajja . To-day; this day. 1. Āj nahīṅ, kal . Prov. Not to-day, to-morrow. This proverb is founded on the story of an ultra-pious Mahomedan who used every night to pray under a tree that God in his love would draw him to himself (Khudā apni muhabbat meṅ īṅch) . Prov. Saying. One night a wag, sitting on a branch overhead, let down a noose with which he began to haul up the devotee. "Not to-day", roared the pious man, "to-morrow." 2. Āj mare, kal dūsrā din . Prov. Dead to-day, to-morrow is but another day.

adv. To day. آج کل āj-kal, Nowadays, to day or to morrow, of late, in a few days. آج کل کرنا āj-kal karnā or آج کل بتانا āj-kal batānā, To procrastinate. آج کي دن āj ke din, To day. آج کي رات āj kī rāt, To night. آج کي روز āj ke roz, To day.
subs: adv. Up to this day; to this time, till now; hitherto; as yet
adv. To-day by day, to day
adj. (f. -ī), To-day's, this, of the present; new, recent, fresh; — āj-kī rāt, This night, to-night
āj-ke roz, adv. To-day — āj-kal, adv. To-day or to-morrow; in a few days, in a day or two, soon; in these days, now-a-days; recently, of late
āj-kal karnā, v.n. To put off from day to day, to procrastinate, delay; to shilly shally, evade, shuffle
adj. (f. -ī), Of the present day, age or generation; recent, fresh
ajahūṅ, adv. (emphatic), 1˚ = āj-tak, q.v. 2˚. This very day, to-day even; just now, even now, at this very time; immediately.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀅𑀚𑁆𑀚 (ajja), from Sanskrit अद्य (adyá).

n. f. 1. A she-goat. Pakhā bīch daī pī gaë, bīte 1 bāj-ahār . 2 Ajā-bhaksh bhejo nahī, 3 duā-das karūṅ ahār . Enigma. My husband pledged his word in two weeks he'd be back—months have gone by— Not ev'n a letter he has sent to me—I'll poison take and die. 2. Illusion; the illusive universe; an epithet of Māyā considered as the wife of the Supreme Being.

more: n. f. اجل the appointed term. 1. Death (maut) ; a term or appointed time (kāl) ; the hour of death; natural death. 2. The term of life; fate; destiny; hand of destiny (Karam) . 3. Met . A creditor who demands the payment of a debt at the appointed time. ajal rasīdah,—giriftah, shāmat kā mārā , adj. 1. Overtaken by fate; doomed; on the point of death. 2. Wretched; unfortunate. ajal-giriftah , A. P. n. A fore-doomed victim or sacrifice.

s. f. The term of life, fate, death, the hour of death, the predestined time of death, a stated time, delay, respite, period for the payment of a debt. اجل رسیده ajal rasīda, Overtaken by fate. اجل گرفته ajal-girifta, Seized by fate, on the point of death, in a situation that leaves no hopes of life, in the jaws of death.
subs: adj. = ajal-rasīda; and s. com. gend. One who is overtaken by fate; one whose hour of death is at hand; doomed person, &c.

def: s.m. = اژدها azhdahā, q.v. s.m. A large serpent, python, boa constrictor ( = ajgar); a dragon.
adj. Very heavy or ponderous; unwieldy.
more: n. m. Old P. اَژدو P. اَژدﮩـا S. अञ a goat, गृ to swallow. lit . The goat-eater. A large serpent or snake; a dragon; boa constrictor; python. 1. Ajgar † kare na chākrī, panchhī kare na kām. Dās Malūkā yoṅ kahe, sab ke Dātā Rām . Malūka Dās. The dragon no one serves, no work the bird performs, But God, who gives to all, doth feed them, saith Malūka Dās. 2. Ajgar ke Dātā, Rām . Prov. God gives to all. [Said by a lazy fellow who spends money but wont work). adj. Very heavy; weighty; unwieldly (bhārī). Ṭolī to ajgar hai, masaktī bhī nahīṅ . The stone is very heavy, I can't move it in least.
s. m. A large serpent, the Boa constrictor.
ex: اجگر ایک مامولی سانپ سے زیادہ خطرناک ہوتا ہے۔
ajgar ek māmūlī sā̃p se zyāda xatarnāk hotā hai.
The python happens to be more dangerous than a run-of-the-mill snake.

def: adj. Wonderful, marvellous, strange, surprising
more: n. f. S. अति + उद्गत, Pālī. achchuggato raised. 1. A phenomenon; a wonder; impossibility. Ek ajgut maiṅ aisā dekhā, ek gadhā do sīṅgh, * Chīṅṭī ke gal paghā dekhā, kheṅcheṅ Arjun, Bhīm . Kabīr. A wondrous thing I saw, a donkey with two horns, A rope around its neck, an ant dragged both Arjun and Bhīm. [This is a satire on the world, where fools are often elevated to power, and small things are accomplished by elaborate contrivances]. 2. Precocity; intelligence beyond one's years. Yeh laṛkinyā ajgut karela, chhawe baras ke ba, sau tak le gin jālis! Bhoj. This little girl does wonders. She is only six years old, and she can count up to a hundred. 3. Flippancy; sauciness; impudence. 4. Wrong; evil; harm; mischief (burāī). Jān bījh ajgut kare, tā se kahā basāë Sabhā Bilās. If one will do wrong knowingly, what can you do with him. 5. Violence; oppression; tyranny; cruelty. Tūṅ baṛ ajgut kaila…
subs: wonder, marvel, prodigy; evil, harm, wrong; violence, outrage, oppression, tyranny ( = غضب g̠aẓab; in these senses ajgut would appear to be identical with ajugat or ajukt = S. ayukta).

ety: S. अ interj.+जी, contr. fr. जीव
def: Interj. (used in addressing, or to call attention, or to express surprise, or contempt), ho there! I say; Sir! O sir! Good sir! Madam! My lady; good woman or good man (forms used by a husband and wife respectively in addressing each other, it being considered disrespectful for them to call each other by name).
more: n. f. 1. A grandmother, E. Nātin sikhāwe ājī ko, ke bārā ḍeoṛhe āṭh . Prov. The granddaughter teaches her grandmother "twelve times one and a half are eight!" (Teach your grandmother to suck eggs). 2. A mother-in-law (sās) . W. Hin. Wom.

An interjection to call or bespeak attention. (In Bengal, it is used to an inferiour or in cases of great intimacy only; but, in the western provinces, it is often addressed to a superiour as a term of respect). Holla! Good sir! Hear me, sir!

def: s.f. (from ājā), A grandmother.

My good sir, let it pass. Are you going to put your mouth in any one's filth? 2. My dear madam; my dear lady; my dear sir. 3. The form of address used by a wife in addressing her husband whose name it is considered disrespectful to take, and vice versâ .

def: adj. def: Gaṛh. adj. S. अजीर्ण, अ not, जृ to be digested. 1. Disagreeing with; indigestible; unwholesome. Jāo, merā khānā ajīran mat karo . 2. Hurtful; pernicious; destructive; fatal. Itnī aql bhī ajīran hoti hai . Prov. You are too sharp, you may cut yourself. 3. Distasteful; tired of; disinclined for; sick of. Roz kī dāl to ajīran ho gaī . 4. Unpleasant; displeasing; disagreeable; vexatious. Itnā haṅsnā bhī aj/?/ran hai . 5. Monotonous; flat; prosy. 6. Persistent; unremitting; incurable. Yeh bukhār to ajiran ho gayā . 7. Tedious; wearisome; tiresome ( pahāṛ ). Āj kā din ajīran ho gayā . 8. Too much (bahut) ; excessive; inordinate; spoilt through excess. Dāl meṅ namak ajīran hai . 9. Excessively hard; strong; difficult. A jīran ko ajīran hī ṭhele. Nahīṅ to sir chauhaṭṭe khele . E. Prov. Let the strong contend with the strong. or your head shall be a foot-ball for the way-farer. 10. Irksome; intolerable; insupportable. Par chand roz ko ham ho jāëṅge ajīran, Yūṅ hī rahā karegā ham se agar takalluf . Inshā. A few days more, and I intol'rable must be to you, If all this labored court'sy you each day renew. 11. Burdensome; heavy; oppressive. Itnā sīnā ajīran hai. ajīran ho'nā , v. n. 1. To be burdensome, tiresome, etc. 2. To grow in abundance; to become a glut (in the market).
s. m. Indigestion; surfeit, flatulence.

ety: act. part. of عجز 'to lack strength, to be powerless,' &c.
def: adj. Lacking strength or power, or ability, powerless, impotent, unable (to do), unequal (to); weak, feeble, helpless; brought low, overcome; lowly, humble; exhausted; dejected; in despair, hopeless; baffled, frustrated
more: adj. عجز was unable. 1. Incapable; unable; crippled; overcome ( be-bas ). 2. Baffled; baulked, frustrated. ājiz ānā yā h. , v. n. To be rendered helpless, Āte haiṅ milte milte jo ājiz parī-rukhāṅ, Dete haiṅ milne-vāloṅ ko ghabrā-ke gāliyāṅ, Tis par bhī lipṭe jāte haiṅ jūṅ guṛ pe makkhīyāṅ, Dāman ke ṭukṛe uṛte haiṅ, phaṭtī haiṅ dhajjiyāṅ, Kyā kyā maze haiṅ Id ke āj Id-gāh meṅ! Nazir. Now beauties fair in helpless close embrace are seen, Though met with showers of abuse these greetings keen Yet still they stick on close as flies on sweets serene, Their garments torn to shreds are scattered all atween Such rapturous joys today illume this Id-gah-scene! ājiz k. , v. a. 1. To render helpless ( zer k. , 2). Zarā se bachche ne ājiz kar dīyā .
subs: or ʻājiz rahnā, or ʻājiz honā (-se), To be helpless (against); to be unequal (to), to be unable to do, to fail or fall short (of, or in respect of); to despair (of), &c.
v.t. To render (one) powerless or helpless; to bring low, to humble, &c.

more: s. f. Bishopʼs weed seed. چوري اجواين chūrī ajwāʼīn, Cleome viscosa: Lin. خراساني اجواين ḵẖurāsānī ajwāʼīn, Hyosciamus niger: Lin. (Qanoon-e-islam).

ety: for ajwad, compar. & super. of جود
def: adj. Better; best; very good; excellent.
more: adj. compar. (from جود) Very good, excellent.

ety: inf. n. of عجز 'to lack strength or power,' &c.
def: s.m. Powerlessness, impotence, weakness, helplessness, submission, wretchedness (ct. ʻājizī).
more: (or iﻌjz ) s. m. Weakness, impotence, submission, wretchedness. عجزدستگاه aﻌjz-dast-gāh, adj. Weak, impotent, wretched.

def: n. m. pl. of جز a part, H. ṭukṛā . 1. Parts; portions; ingredients; component parts; constituents; members; elements ( tat ). 2. Sections of the Qurān , of which there are thirty. ajzāë-tahajjī , n. Ped, Syllables.
s. m. plur. (of جز) Parts, portions.
s.m. Parts, portions, divisions, sections; sections of the Qorʼān; feet (of a verse); constituent parts (of a thing), elements; members; ingredients (of any compound or mixture).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اجزا (ajzā), from Arabic أَجْزَاء (ʔajzāʔ).

def: s.m. Curled, flowered, gigantic swallow-wort, Calotropis gigantea (syn. madār).
more: n. m. S. अर्क. Curled, flowered gigantic swallow-wort, celebrated among native practitioners for its many medicinal qualities; Calotropis gigantea; Saccharum officinarum . Wat. "Rādhā," "Rādhā," raṭat haiṅ āk ḍhāk, aur kair, Tulsī, yā Brij bhūm meṅ kahā Rām sūṅ bair? Tulsī Dās, "Rādhā," "Rādhā," āk ḍhāk, & kair trees call on so: O Tulsī! can then Brij land be of Rām the foe? āk kī buṛhyā , The inner, light, soft substance of the fruit of the ākh tree. Bajuz mūe sufaid us meṅ nahīṅ kuchh; Nahīṅ jūṅ ākh kī buṛhyā kahīṅ kuchh . Saudā. Besides white hair naught's left in him at all, No substance save what's in a light pith ball.
1. (Sans. अर्क ) s. m. Curled flowered gigantick swallow-wort. (Asclepias gigantea). 2. (Sans. इक्षु ) s. m. A sprout of sugar-cane.

def: s.m. A sprout of sugar-cane (cf. ankur).
s.m. Lord, master, owner ( = اغا āg̠ā. q.v.). s.m. Curled, flowered, gigantic swallow-wort, Calotropis gigantea (syn. madār).
n. m. Master; owner; an employer; lord.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آقا (āqā), from Turkic. Compare Turkish ağa.

def: = H اکا akkā, s.m. Lit. 'elder brother' ( = آغا dg̠ā); brother; friend, dear friend; dear sir.
more: Mah. n. m. Brother; friend; dear; dear friend; dear sir.

def: s.m. pl. (of اکبر akbar), The great, grandees, people of rank
more: adj. plur. (of اکبر) The great, people of rank. اکابرواصاغر akābir wa aṣāg̠ẖir, High and low, rich and poor.
subs: High and low, rich and poor.

def: academy
wety: Assimilated from English academy, from French académie, from Latin acadēmīa, from Ancient Greek Ἀκαδημία (Akadēmía).

def: academic
s.m. pl. (of ʻaqīda), Articles of faith, religious tenets.
s.m. One of the sacks or baskets of a pannier, &c. = اکها akkhā or ākhā, q.v.
s.m. Hurt, injury, loss, detriment; wrong, impropriety; uselessness; — adj. Useless, unprofitable, vain, worthless ( = akājī); — adv. Uselessly, unprofitably, in vain.
more: adj. S. अकार्य्य, , from अ not, कृ to use. Vain; useless; unprofitable; worthless. —adv. Uselessly; in vain; unprofitably. Bīte jāe haiṅ, bite jāe haiṅ janam akāj re . Nānak Passing away, passing away in vain your life!
s. m. 1. Hurt, injury, loss, detriment, prejudice. 2. Uselessness.

def: adj. & s.m. Detrimental, injurious, retarding, obstructive; useless; — an obstructive person or thing, a useless person; an impediment; a retarder.
more: adj. Useless (animal, thing, or person); a retarder.

more: n. m. Acacia; the expressed juice of the fruit of the Acacia tree, or of the qalqal .
s. Acacia, the expressed juice of the fruit of the acacia tree, or of the قلقل ḳilḳil.

def: uncle; respectful term of address for any elder man
s.m. = آکار ākār, q.v. s.m. (in Gram.) The letter अ a.

adj. H. ek one, P. khur , H. khā , S. खाद् to eat. lit . One who eats by himself. 1. Selfish; self-seeking; greedy. 2. Unsociable; unsocial; inhospitable. akal khurā, ikal khurā , n. m. ikal khurī , n. f. 1. A selfish, self-seeking, greedy person. 2. A jealous, envious person. 1. Akal khurā kisī ko dekh nahīṅ saktā . 2. Akal khurā jag se burā . Prov. For bad men though the world you scan, You'll ne'er find one like the envious man.

s.m. An inopportune or unseasonable time; untimeliness, unseasonableness; a bad season, a season of scarcity; drought, scarcity, dearth, famine; extremity, pinch; adj. Untimely, unseasonable, inopportune, premature
more: W. n. m. H. a , priv., kāl time, hence untimeliness, unseasonableness. 1. A bad season; a season of scarcity. 2. Drought; scarcity; a general scarcity; dearth. akāl maut , n. m. Premature death; sudden death; unnatural death ( bin āī maut ).
1. s. m. Unseasonableness, famine, scarcity, extremity, pinch. 2. adj. Unseasonable, premature, untimely. अकालवृष्टि akāla-vr̤ish̤ṭi, Untimely rain.
subs: s.m. Unseasonable fruit, fruit produced out of season
s.f. Untimely or premature death; sudden death

subs: (ikal-), -khurā, adj. (f. -ī), Eating alone or by oneself; greedy, selfish; unsociable, inhospitable; — s.m. A greedy person; selfish person; jealous or envious person.

s.m. pl. (of اقليم iqlīm), Climes, regions.
more: s. plur. (of اقلیم) Climes, climates, regions.

adj. compar. (of قلیل) Less, least. اقل واکثر aḳall o aks̤ar, Least and most, every particular.

minority
